hero.headlinePart2: into sales, support and ops — built around your processes
hero.subhead: 30-minute audit → in 48 hours, a map of 5 spots where AI pays back in 3 months. No hype, no boxes. Won't pay back — we'll say so ourselves and pass.
hero.proofLine: Dubai · 8 months in real production · dozens of automations
hero.trustLine: Hands-on · you talk to the people writing the code · access from day one

aiLeaders.title: They pulled ahead. It's not magic — it's AI and automation.
aiLeaders.lead: Ten companies that didn't just adopt the tech — they used it to break away from competitors. Their budgets run into billions, yours into thousands. That's fine: don't watch the numbers, watch the pattern. They stopped doing repetitive work by hand long ago — and your slice of that logic can ship in weeks, not years.

companies.name: Klarna
companies.oneLineEdge: AI assistant does the work of 700 support agents — and answers in 2 minutes instead of 11.
companies.whatTheyAutomated: Customer support across 23 countries handled by a single AI assistant. Internal comms, marketing, and legal run through a corporate GPT portal used daily by 90% of staff.
companies.howTheyUseAI: An OpenAI-powered assistant handles chat in 35+ languages 24/7, classifies tickets, processes returns and disputes. The back office plugs into GPT through Klarna's internal Kiki tool.
companies.leadVsCompetitors: 2.3M conversations in the first month — two-thirds of all CS traffic. Resolution time fell from 11 minutes to under two. Repeat contacts down 25%. Estimated profit impact: $40M a year.

companies.name: Netflix
companies.oneLineEdge: 80% of all viewing comes from recommendations, not search.
companies.whatTheyAutomated: Content discovery, personalized poster artwork per viewer, video encoding and delivery, A/B-tested promotions.
companies.howTheyUseAI: Collaborative filtering plus content embeddings. A personally ranked home screen for every viewer. Models predict retention before a show even airs.
companies.leadVsCompetitors: 80% of watch hours are algorithm-initiated. The recommender is estimated to retain about $1B in revenue per year — competitors haven't caught up.

companies.name: Amazon
companies.oneLineEdge: Recommendations drive 35% of sales. 750,000+ robots run the warehouses.
companies.whatTheyAutomated: Warehouse logistics after the $775M Kiva Systems acquisition; catalog recommendations; dynamic pricing; demand forecasting per SKU; last-mile delivery.
companies.howTheyUseAI: Item-to-item collaborative filtering on the home page and product cards. Deep learning per SKU. Computer vision in fulfillment centers for picking. LLM assistants for shoppers and sellers (Rufus).
companies.leadVsCompetitors: 35% of revenue attributed to recommendations. Robots doubled storage density and dropped operating cost by ~20%. ~1.6M packages a day in the US alone.

companies.name: JPMorgan
companies.oneLineEdge: AI parses 12,000 contracts in seconds — saving 360,000 lawyer-hours a year.
companies.whatTheyAutomated: Parsing and classification of commercial loan agreements (project COIN). Compliance checks. KYC procedures.
companies.howTheyUseAI: NLP, ML, and image recognition extract clauses, classify language, and flag risky or non-standard terms in contracts.
companies.leadVsCompetitors: 360,000 lawyer-hours a year — that's 41 person-years of work. Compliance errors down 80%. Estimated savings: $144M a year.

companies.name: Spotify
companies.oneLineEdge: Discover Weekly has been streamed 100B+ times — a fresh playlist for every user.
companies.whatTheyAutomated: Music discovery, an AI DJ, segmentation of billions of daily events for ads, AI-voiced track intros.
companies.howTheyUseAI: Collaborative filtering plus audio-NLP embeddings. Neural ranking. Generative voice for the AI DJ. Prompted Playlist turns a text request into a playlist.
companies.leadVsCompetitors: 100B+ Discover Weekly streams. 678M users. Apple Music and Amazon Music trail on personalization — Spotify keeps widening the gap with LLM features.

companies.name: Stripe
companies.oneLineEdge: Radar blocks fraud in <100 ms across billions of network transactions.
companies.whatTheyAutomated: Fraud detection. Payment authorization. Adaptive rules. Payment success rate optimization.
companies.howTheyUseAI: A multi-head neural classifier on top of cross-network signals. Adaptive rules combine the ML score with issuer responses. Real-time scoring on every transaction.
companies.leadVsCompetitors: Radar customers see 32% less fraud on average. The new multi-head model adds another 30% on top. +1.3 pp to payment success rate. SEPA fraud −42%, ACH −20%.

companies.name: Uber
companies.oneLineEdge: DeepETA returns an arrival prediction in ~3 ms — across 30M matches per minute.
companies.whatTheyAutomated: ETA computation, driver–rider matching, dynamic pricing, routing, trip moderation.
companies.howTheyUseAI: Self-attention deep learning sits on top of the routing engine to predict the gap between estimated and real arrival time. Tree-based ensembles match trips on 1,000+ features.
companies.leadVsCompetitors: Median latency 3.25 ms, p95 4 ms. Over 30M match-pair predictions per minute. Trained on 1.4B historical queries. Sharper ETAs cut cancellations and raise marketplace margin.

companies.name: Booking.com
companies.oneLineEdge: ML ranks 1.5M properties personally for every guest.
companies.whatTheyAutomated: Search ranking, personalized recommendations, dynamic pricing, support, and trip planning via the GenAI Trip Planner.
companies.howTheyUseAI: A multi-stage ensemble of ML models (pCTR, pCVR, perceived quality). Personal relevance by date, passport, group composition. An LLM trip planner on top of OpenAI.
companies.leadVsCompetitors: About 150 ML models in production. 1.5M properties and 125M reviews as a training pool. No other OTA personalizes property position per search at this scale.

companies.name: Duolingo
companies.oneLineEdge: GPT-4 explains mistakes and runs role-play conversations — Max tier at $30/month.
companies.whatTheyAutomated: Mistake explanations (Explain My Answer), role-plays, AI-character video calls, lesson generation.
companies.howTheyUseAI: GPT-4 sits on top of Duolingo's product layer with curated prompts, safety filters, and per-learner difficulty.
companies.leadVsCompetitors: DAU 34.1M (+59% YoY in Q2 2024). Revenue +41% to $178M. Max-tier reached ~5% of subscribers right after launch. +15% completion rate among Explain My Answer users.

companies.name: Revolut
companies.oneLineEdge: Account opening in ~6 minutes vs. days at a bank. AI fraud detection across billions of transactions.
companies.whatTheyAutomated: Onboarding and KYC over the phone. Anti-fraud and scam detection. Biometrics (Face ID + selfie). Chat support.
companies.howTheyUseAI: ML reads the context around transactions and messages: it spots and blocks the «met someone online → money to a stranger → asked for a code» pattern before the transfer goes through. Biometric models verify the face.
companies.leadVsCompetitors: 50M+ customers globally. AI scam detection has stopped tens of millions of pounds in fraudulent transfers. Valuation around $45B — higher than many traditional banks where opening an account still takes weeks.

closing.title: They pulled ahead because they started early. Your turn now — at your own scale.
closing.body: The gap isn't budgets or geniuses — it's that they stopped doing by hand what machines have done for years. Next up — six areas where we already have proven payback patterns for small and mid-size business.

wizard.title: Danila · MagicWork AI demo
wizard.subtitle: In 60 seconds I'll show you what's worth automating in your business. This isn't real GPT — it's a demo: 4 questions, I return a personal recommendation. The exact plan comes from the free audit.
wizard.intro: Hi 👋 This is Danila, founder of MagicWork — well, my AI demo. In 60 seconds I'll show you what to automate first in your business — no budget talk, no jargon. Shall we?

step1.q: First — what kind of business do you run?

step2.q: Where do you lose the most time right now?

options.label: Inbound leads and calls — can't reply fast enough

step4.q: When would you like to launch automation at your company?

options.label: Just researching the market for now

preview.preface: Okay, here's what I see for you:
preview.ask: Want me to send the full recommendation and payback math? Drop a contact — Danila from MagicWork will reach out within 24 hours.
preview.skipLabel: Just show me the recommendation, skip contact

contact.title: Where should I send it?
contact.subtitle: Name and any channel that works — Telegram, email or phone. We'll write where it's easiest for you.
contact.success: Got it. Danila will reach out within 24 hours with the full math.
contact.error: Something broke. Message us on Telegram directly — link below.

result.title: What I'd automate first at your place
result.ctaBody: 30 minutes on a free audit (Zoom or face-to-face in Dubai Marina), we map your processes and compute payback in hours × loaded rate. In 48 hours — a PDF with the plan, yours to keep.
result.askHuman: Talk to a human on Telegram
result.disclaimer: Simplified recommendation based on typical patterns. We get specific on the audit.

recs.horeca_leads: Evening reservations slip away while your host is busy — the guest just walks to the place next door. An AI assistant in WhatsApp takes the booking in 30 seconds, writes it to your POS, and sends reminders. Recovers 60–80% of missed calls. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.horeca_docs: Supplier orders, invoices, shift sheets — your manager loses 2–3 hours a day on paperwork instead of guests. The AI assistant reads invoice photos, matches them against orders, posts to your accounting, and builds shift reports. Saves 40–50 hours per month. Payback in 3 months.
recs.horeca_ops: The bar runs out of truffle oil — and you learn about it from a guest mid-service. The AI assistant tracks stock from receipts, sends your buyer a daily 'order today' list, and warns the chef about expiry. Cuts the stop-list 2–3x. Payback in 3–4 months.
recs.horeca_support: Guests message Instagram and WhatsApp at 11 PM — 'vegan options?', 'dogs allowed?', 'parking?'. The AI assistant answers 80% instantly and hands tougher ones to your manager in the morning. Chat-to-booking conversion up 25–35%. Payback in 2 months.
recs.ecom_leads: Out of 100 visitors, 1–2 buy — the rest 'think it over' and never come back. The AI assistant messages cart-abandoners on WhatsApp, handles objections, and closes the order in chat. Recovers 15–25% of abandoned carts. Payback in 2 months.
recs.ecom_docs: Invoices, shipments, returns, labeling — your accountant drowns in spreadsheets and loses days reconciling. The AI assistant pulls invoices from email, posts them to your accounting, and prepares closing docs for clients. Saves 60–80 hours per month. Payback in 3 months.
recs.ecom_ops: An order comes in — the warehouse learns about it an hour later, the courier two, the customer is in the dark until evening. The AI assistant routes it to the warehouse, assigns a courier, and sends the customer a tracking number automatically. Fulfillment time -40%, errors -60%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.ecom_support: 'Where's my order?' — 60% of all support requests, eating 5 minutes per ticket. The AI assistant reads order status from your CRM, replies in WhatsApp/Telegram in 10 seconds, and escalates only the real problems. Cuts support load 70%. Payback in 2 months.
recs.agency_leads: Leads come from your site, Instagram, referrals — your manager only calls back a third of them, the rest go cold. The AI assistant messages within the first minute, qualifies by budget and need, and books a call. Lead-to-call conversion +40%. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.agency_docs: Contract, proposal, brief, act — your PM cobbles them together from templates, losing 6–8 hours a week. From a single chat conversation, the AI assistant assembles the contract and proposal on your template, fills in client details, and sends for signing. Frees 30+ hours per month per PM. Payback in 2 months.
recs.agency_ops: Client tasks are scattered — some in Asana, some in chat, deadlines slip. The AI assistant pulls client requests from chat, routes them to the right project, and nudges the team on deadlines. Missed deadlines -50%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.agency_support: A client asks 'how's our reel coming?' at 9 PM — your manager replies in the morning and trust erodes. The AI assistant knows the status of every project, replies instantly, and escalates only what matters. Client retention +20–25%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.manufacturing_leads: Inquiries arrive by email and through your site — your sales rep handles them in 1–2 days while the prospect goes to competitors. The AI assistant captures the request, clarifies the spec, builds a rough estimate from your price list, and hands sales a warm lead. Conversion +30%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.manufacturing_docs: Specs, waybills, invoices, customs forms — your docs team drowns and shipments get delayed. The AI assistant assembles the document package per order, checks it against the production task, and prepares everything for the client. Processing time -60%. Payback in 3–4 months.
recs.manufacturing_ops: An order comes in — the shop floor learns 2 days later, raw materials aren't bought, the deadline slips. The AI assistant pushes the order to production, checks raw-material stock, and triggers supplier requests. Missed deadlines -35–45%. Payback in 4 months.
recs.manufacturing_support: A client calls to check shipment status — your rep spends half an hour finding the answer and calls back later. The AI assistant sees production status and replies in WhatsApp instantly. Routine calls -60%, repeat orders +20%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.realestate_leads: 70% of listing inquiries come in the evening when the agent is on a viewing. The AI assistant takes calls and chats 24/7, qualifies the buyer (budget, area, timeline), and books a viewing in the agent's calendar. Inquiry-to-viewing +40%. Payback in 2 months.
recs.realestate_docs: Lease agreement, handover act, inventory, statements — the agent loses 4–5 hours per deal. The AI assistant builds the package from templates tailored to client and property, then sends it for signature via DocuSign. Deal paperwork time -3x. Payback in 3 months.
recs.realestate_ops: Listings are scattered across spreadsheets, chats, and CRMs — the agent forgets which owner already approved a viewing. The AI assistant keeps listings in one place, nudges owners, and syncs with portals (Property Finder, Bayut, Dubizzle). Frees 15+ hours per week. Payback in 3 months.
recs.realestate_support: After closing, the client asks 'how do I pay utilities?', 'when's the handyman coming?' — the agent is busy, the client gets frustrated. The AI assistant answers post-deal questions from the property's knowledge base and escalates only real issues. NPS +25 points. Payback in 3 months.
recs.b2bsaas_leads: A B2B inbound lead waits 2–4 hours for a reply — by then they're cold and writing your competitor. The AI assistant replies within a minute, runs qualifying questions (team size, use case, budget), and books a demo on the right AE's calendar. Demo conversion +50%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.b2bsaas_docs: Contracts, NDAs, DPAs, invoices — legal and sales bounce files over email for weeks. The AI assistant builds the contract from a template using deal terms, fills in client data, and sends for signature. 'Verbal yes' to signature drops from 2 weeks to 2 days. Payback in 2 months.
recs.b2bsaas_ops: Onboarding a new client is a series of emails, guides, and videos the AE sends manually — half the clients don't open them. The AI assistant walks the client through each step in chat, answers questions, and escalates to CSM only when there's a real problem. Churn -15–20%. Payback in 4 months.
recs.b2bsaas_support: Support tickets scale with your customer base — hiring more agents is expensive. The AI assistant resolves 70% of routine tickets (password resets, billing, user invites) and hands the rest to a human. Saves 2 full-time support hires. Payback in 3 months.
recs.clinic_leads: In a clinic, 40% of bookings are lost because the front-desk admin is busy with a patient in person. The AI assistant takes bookings in WhatsApp, matches a doctor and slot, and confirms the first visit in 1 minute. Recovers 50–70% of missed inquiries. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.clinic_docs: Consent forms, treatment agreements, discharge summaries — the admin spends 15–20 minutes printing and signing with each patient. The AI assistant prepares documents ahead of the visit, the patient signs from their phone. Paperwork time -4x. Payback in 3 months.
recs.clinic_ops: Patients forget appointments — no-show rates hit 30% and doctors sit idle. The AI assistant sends WhatsApp reminders 24 hours and 1 hour ahead, offers rescheduling, and fills freed slots from the waitlist. No-show -40–50%. Payback in 2 months.
recs.clinic_support: After a visit patients ask 'can I take this with alcohol?', 'when do I remove the bandage?' — doctors reply between appointments or not at all. The AI assistant answers routine post-visit questions from the medical knowledge base and escalates serious cases to the doctor. Patient loyalty +30%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.beauty_leads: In a salon, 50% of bookings come through Instagram DMs and WhatsApp — the stylist is with a client and replies an hour later. The AI assistant books via WhatsApp in 30 seconds, matches stylist and slot, and sends confirmation. Recovers 60% of missed bookings. Payback in 2 months.
recs.beauty_docs: Client cards, supply logs, stylist timesheets — the admin keeps them in a notebook or Excel. The AI assistant fills client cards from bookings, tracks supply usage automatically, and calculates stylist payroll at month-end. Saves the admin 20–30 hours. Payback in 3 months.
recs.beauty_ops: Clients visit every 2 months — they forget the next appointment and drift to competitors. The AI assistant nudges them in WhatsApp ('time for a trim'), suggests a slot, and books it. Win-back rate 25–35% on dormant clients. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.beauty_support: 'How much for coloring?', 'can I get extensions with my hair type?', 'is there parking?' — the admin answers the same questions 30 times a day. The AI assistant handles 80% from the salon's knowledge base and routes complex ones to a stylist. Frees the admin for in-salon clients. Payback in 2 months.
recs.legal_leads: A law firm lead waits half a day for a reply — and ends up with a competitor. The AI assistant takes the inquiry in chat, clarifies the case and urgency, and books a paid consultation with the right lawyer. Lead-to-paid-consult conversion +35%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.legal_docs: Contract, claim, demand letter, power of attorney — a lawyer spends 2–3 hours assembling each from templates. From a client conversation, the AI assistant drafts the document on your template, fills in the data, and hands it to the lawyer for review. Frees 40–50 hours per month per lawyer. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.legal_ops: Cases tracked in Excel, court deadlines kept in your head — something gets missed and fines follow. The AI assistant keeps cases in one place, reminds you of hearing dates and filing deadlines, and assigns tasks to paralegals. Missed deadlines -80%. Payback in 4 months.
recs.legal_support: Clients ask 'how's my case?' every week — the lawyer spends more time answering than working the case. The AI assistant knows the status of every case, replies to clients, and escalates only material updates. Frees 10–15 hours per week per lawyer. Payback in 3 months.
recs.education_leads: For an online school, 3–5 of every 100 interested visitors actually buy — no one follows up with the rest. The AI assistant messages on WhatsApp within 5 minutes, handles objections on program and price, and books a free trial lesson. Sign-up conversion +30–40%. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.education_docs: Student contracts, completion certificates, installment agreements — the admin and accountant drown in paperwork. The AI assistant prepares the contract and payment docs from the course sign-up and sends them to the parent to sign. Onboarding paperwork drops from 2 days to 30 minutes. Payback in 3 months.
recs.education_ops: A student skips a lesson — the coordinator finds out 2 days later, by which point motivation is gone. The AI assistant tracks attendance and homework deadlines, messages student and parent when something's missed, and books a catch-up. Course drop-off -25%. Payback in 4 months.
recs.education_support: 'When's the next lesson?', 'where do I submit homework?', 'can I pay in installments?' — the coordinator answers the same things daily. The AI assistant resolves 80% of routine questions and routes complex ones to a human. Frees coordinators for students who actually need them. Payback in 2 months.
recs.logistics_leads: In logistics, a client asks 'quote me Dubai to Almaty, 200 kg' — the manager spends half a day, the client goes elsewhere. The AI assistant gathers cargo details in chat, returns a tariff-based estimate, and books a call for the final offer. Conversion +35%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.logistics_docs: Bill of lading, invoice, CMR, customs declaration — your docs team drowns and cargo sits at customs. The AI assistant assembles the shipment package, cross-checks data, and prepares customs paperwork. Shipment processing time -50%. Payback in 3–4 months.
recs.logistics_ops: A truck leaves — the client calls every 2 hours asking 'where's my cargo?'. The dispatcher answers instead of dispatching. The AI assistant sees GPS position and sends the client automatic updates with ETA. Cuts dispatcher calls 70%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.logistics_support: Claims about damage, delays, paperwork — a manager spends hours per case. The AI assistant pulls shipment data (tracking, photos, signatures), drafts a client response, and escalates only disputed cases. Cuts routine claims handling 60%. Payback in 4 months.
recs.construction_leads: A renovation prospect calls 'how much for a 2-bedroom?' — the manager takes a note, shows up a week later, by which point the neighboring crew got the job. The AI assistant gathers the basics (size, condition, materials), gives a price range, and books a site visit in the next available slot. Inquiry-to-visit conversion +40%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.construction_docs: Estimate, contractor agreement, hidden-works act, progress reports — the foreman cobbles them together in the evening, losing 10 hours a week. The AI assistant builds the estimate and contract from project parameters, prepares stage-completion acts, and sends them for signing. Frees 40+ hours per month per foreman. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.construction_ops: Tiles run out on site — you find out when the worker stops. Each day of idle crew costs serious money. The AI assistant tracks material usage from on-site photos and triggers procurement for tomorrow's needs. Crew downtime -50%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.construction_support: A client asks 'when will the kitchen be done?' — the foreman is on site and replies in the evening, trust erodes. The AI assistant reads stage status from the schedule, replies to the client with on-site photos, and escalates only real problems. Repeat business +25%. Payback in 4 months.
recs.hr_leads: An HR agency client says 'we need a senior dev' — the manager takes a day, the best candidates are already at competitors. The AI assistant takes the request, clarifies stack and conditions, and hands the recruiter a finished brief. Request-to-response time drops from 1 day to 1 hour. Payback in 3 months.
recs.hr_docs: Client contract, NDA, offer letter, candidate agreement — the recruiter spends half a day assembling them. The AI assistant builds the package per role and candidate, then sends for signature via DocuSign. Frees 20–30 hours per month per recruiter. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.hr_ops: 100 applicants for one role — the recruiter reviews 30, the rest go cold, and the best may be among them. The AI assistant screens resumes, filters out clear non-fits, runs a chat-based initial interview, and hands the recruiter the top 10. Time-to-hire -50%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.hr_support: Candidates ask 'when's the call?', 'did I get it?', 'when's the offer?' — the recruiter replies in the evening and the candidate walks. The AI assistant knows every candidate's status and replies on WhatsApp/Telegram instantly. Candidate drop-off across the funnel -20%. Payback in 3 months.
recs.auto_leads: A customer asks 'how much for a service on a 2019 Camry?' — the manager replies 2 hours later, the customer is already booked at the shop across the street. The AI assistant takes the request in WhatsApp, prices it from your sheet, and books an open slot. Inquiry-to-booking +45%. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.auto_docs: Work order, intake form, warranty card — the service advisor fills them by hand, 20 minutes per car. The AI assistant prepares the work order from the booking and client history, the advisor just reviews. Intake time -3x. Payback in 3 months.
recs.auto_ops: A customer no-shows the appointment — the mechanic sits idle, the slot is lost. The AI assistant reminds in WhatsApp 24 hours and 2 hours ahead, offers rescheduling, and fills the slot from the waitlist. No-show -50%. Payback in 2 months.
recs.auto_support: Three months after service, the customer is due again — no one reminds them, they go to a chain shop. The AI assistant messages 'time for an oil change' based on mileage and history and books the slot. Win-back rate 30–40% on dormant customers. Payback in 3 months.
recs.other_leads: If your niche isn't listed — the lead leak is the same for most: a customer messages in the evening or on a weekend, gets no reply until morning, and walks. The AI assistant replies on WhatsApp/Telegram/Instagram in 30 seconds, 24/7, qualifies, and books the next step. Conversion +30–50%. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.other_docs: In any business, paperwork is hours of manual work that machines handle easily. The AI assistant builds your contract/invoice/act from a template using CRM or chat data, then sends for signature. Frees 20–40 hours per month for whoever was handling it. Payback in 2–3 months.
recs.other_ops: If you can't pinpoint where operations leak — it's almost always repetition: identical tasks, data shuffled between tools, manual follow-up. The AI assistant takes that routine off your plate: tracks statuses, sends reminders, assigns tasks. Frees 15–25 hours per week across the team. Payback in 3 months.
recs.other_support: Support eats into time of people who should be doing your core work. The AI assistant handles 70–80% of routine questions from your knowledge base and escalates only the complex ones. Cuts load and brings response times from hours to seconds. Payback in 2–3 months.

fab.label: AI demo · 60 sec
fab.tooltip: 60 seconds to see what you can automate

explainer.title: What an AI employee actually is
explainer.lead: Not a "neural net for pictures," not a "Telegram bot for $10/month." An employee in the literal sense: works to your playbook, remembers context, reports back. Just doesn't sleep and doesn't ask for a paycheck.

cards.title: A program-employee that follows your rules
cards.body: Understands what's asked in plain language. Asks follow-ups, checks the CRM, looks up stock, escalates to a human when the case isn't standard.

items: Replies to customers at 10pm like a sales manager
Fills the CRM while you sleep
Builds a report in a minute instead of a day
Tracks deadlines and deals slipping through cracks
Make calls with no rule attached
Replace a lawyer on a $1M deal
Run without your oversight for the first 2 weeks
Guarantee 0% errors — we catch them with guardrails

pain.title: Sound familiar?

items.title: Half the day lost to copy-paste
items.body: A manager moves an order from email into CRM, then into inventory, then into a report. Every single day, on loop. Then we wonder why no one has time to think.

items.title: Lead messages at 10pm. By 10:05 they're with a competitor
items.body: By the time you reply in the morning, they've already signed with whoever answered first. Ad budget — gone. Lead — gone. Twice.

items.title: Everyone has their own number. No one is right
items.body: CRM says one thing, inventory another, accounting a third. The meeting starts with «well, in my spreadsheet…». Half a day on reconciling, zero on actual work.

items.title: Want more revenue — hire more people
items.body: More clients — more managers. More managers — more chaos: hiring, training, churn. Revenue goes up, you burn out.

pain.answer: We'll look at your processes. We'll tell you straight: what to automate now, what later, what not to touch at all. No upsell.

approach.title: We don't sell boxes
approach.lead: We don't repackage SaaS, we don't bend your processes around someone else's template. Every project is built around your operating logic. But after 8+ months in production and dozens of scenarios, we have working patterns: what sticks in HoReCa, what sticks in e-commerce, what sticks in B2B. We adapt them to your case.

principles.title: One client — one project
principles.body: No two businesses are alike — neither are our builds. Every solution is designed from scratch around your processes, people and tools.

principles.title: Stack picked for the task, not for us
principles.body: n8n, Make, Python, low-code, custom — we choose what fits your case best. We don't push a technology because it's convenient for us.

principles.title: Won't pay back — we'll say so ourselves
principles.body: We don't talk you into automating for the invoice. Sometimes the right answer is "leave it to the humans." We'll say it honestly and explain why.

approach.bridge: To have a reference point — first, a look at how the leaders already use this. Then back to the six areas where our patterns pay back.

services.title: Six areas where the patterns are already there
services.lead: Six areas with proven solutions and clear payback math. Stack, logic and scenarios — rebuilt around your team.
services.framing: What we see most often in these niches
services.selectorHint: We'll show the patterns we've already shipped — and where they paid back first
services.individualNote: These are starting points. The actual set we'll shape around your processes on the audit.

niches.label: Restaurant · HoReCa
niches.summary: Orders, bookings, supplies and analytics — no managers on the phone. Typically takes out 60–80% of inbound calls.
items: Order intake via WhatsApp/Telegram, auto-saved to POS
Table reservations with confirmations and reminders
Stock control, auto-purchase orders to suppliers, demand forecast

niches.label: E-commerce · shop
niches.summary: AI shopping assistant + auto-catalog + synced inventory. One person handles a catalog that used to need a team.
items: AI shopping assistant on site: helps pick the product and closes the sale
Auto-generated product cards, descriptions and SEO across the catalog
Sync inventory ↔ marketplaces ↔ CRM in real time

niches.label: Agency · services
niches.summary: AI intake handles incoming, proposals and onboarding. Account managers only deal with warm leads.
items: AI intake assistant qualifies inbound: asks about budget and scope on its own
Auto-generated proposals and contracts from CRM templates
Client reports: metrics, screenshots, status — weekly, hands-off

niches.label: Manufacturing
niches.summary: Documents, requests, QC and plan-vs-actual without paper or double bookkeeping.
items: Material and purchase requests: routing, approvals, deadlines
Digital document workflow: −90% time on approvals (our case)
Photo-based QC with AI: defects caught right on the line

services.ctaHint: Don't see your niche? Describe the task — we'll figure it out.

case.title: Digital document workflow for a city administration
case.lede: We delivered a full incoming/outgoing document handling system: routing, approvals, deadline control, executor notifications. In production from month one.

alsoBuilt.title: Marketing websites
alsoBuilt.body: Landing pages and corporate sites with form-to-CRM and messenger integrations baked in.

alsoBuilt.title: Telegram bots
alsoBuilt.body: Bots that talk to customers, collect data, write into CRM and sheets — for services, bookings and support.

alsoBuilt.title: Turnkey integrations
alsoBuilt.body: n8n / Make scenarios linking CRM, inventory, payments and notifications — zero manual steps.

process.title: Four stages. After each — an artifact and a go/no-go decision.

steps.title: Discovery & audit
steps.output: Process map, ROI math, PDF in your hands
deliverables: Map of 5–7 of your processes, prioritized by payback
ROI math: hours × loaded rate, in EUR or AED
List of what NOT to automate (and why)
PDF is yours to keep — even if we don't move forward
steps.body: We get on a call and dig into your processes — no templates, no canned answers. In 48 hours you have a document you can decide on yourself — with us or without.

steps.title: Prototype on your logic
steps.output: Working prototype on your real data
steps.body: We build a minimal working version on your real data and scenarios. You see the impact on concrete cases — before signing the bigger scope.

steps.title: Rollout
steps.body: We scale the prototype, integrate with existing systems, train the team, and document everything. Access — yours from day one.

steps.title: Support
steps.output: SLA, monitoring, new scenarios on demand
steps.body: We monitor stability, adjust to process changes, and add new scenarios. Walking away from us is painless — the stack is open, not proprietary.

process.guarantee: Between stages there's a kill switch. We don't move forward until you've seen the result of the previous one.

guarantees.title: What's written into the contract
guarantees.lead: No "we'll improve efficiency" — concrete obligations on paper. We deliver what we promise, or we refund.

items.title: Payment per stage
items.body: No next stage — no payment. No upfront fee for the full scope.

items.title: KPIs in the SLA
items.body: Concrete numbers in the contract: response time, accuracy, uptime. Not "we'll improve the team".

items.title: Access from day one
items.body: Logins, docs, repo — yours from day one. Walking away from us is painless by design.

items.title: Open stack
items.body: n8n, Python, standard APIs — any engineer can pick it up. No proprietary language of ours.

highlight.title: Prototype doesn't pay back — we refund the prototype fee
highlight.body: We fix the payback criterion in the contract upfront — as a number, not a vibe: hours × loaded rate, concrete metrics, a measurement window. After 1–3 weeks of prototyping we compare against real data. Doesn't match — we refund the prototype fee. Anything debatable resolves in the client's favor.

team.title: Two of us — both build
team.body: We moved to Dubai to deliver real value to local businesses: we ship what pays back and talk you out of what doesn't. No middlemen — the person on the call is the one who writes the code. Every project is run personally, from first call to release. On larger engagements we bring in trusted partners — but the interface stays the same: you talk to the people accountable for the result.

faq.title: Common questions

items.q: We tried automation before — got burned. What's different here?
items.a: Most failures come from "the box", not from the tech. We don't go into a big rollout without a prototype. 1–3 weeks, on your data, on your scenarios — if there's no impact, we don't move forward. You pay per stage, with a kill switch between them.

items.q: We already have a bot in ManyChat / Intercom / WhatsApp — what's different here?
items.a: Off-the-shelf bots run on "if X — then Y" scripts. They're fast and cheap as long as customer requests stay inside the script. We build an AI employee that handles requests outside the script, remembers conversation context, and plugs straight into your CRM and ops. If your current bot closes 100% of cases — you don't need us. If it's 30–50% — let's see what catches the rest.

items.q: What if the AI employee tells a customer something dumb or hallucinated — who's on the hook?
items.a: The cost of an error decides the architecture. For customer-facing flows — RAG over your data, explicit guardrails, escalation to a human. Where hallucination is expensive, we don't use AI at all — we go deterministic.

items.q: What about privacy? We have NDA, GDPR, internal policies.
items.a: By default we deploy inside your perimeter — data doesn't leave. We sign NDA. AI models can run locally or via private endpoints (Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, on-prem).

items.q: What if OpenAI access is cut or sanctions hit our region?
items.a: We're not locked into one model. From day one we design for a provider swap: we can switch to Azure OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, or self-host open models (Llama, Qwen, Mistral) on your own servers. A swap typically takes a week. Business logic and integrations stay — only the "brain" changes.

items.q: What if you disappear after the launch? We'll be left with a broken system.
items.a: Documentation and access — yours from day one. We pick a stack your future engineer can pick up — n8n, Python, not our proprietary language. Support SLA is fixed. Walking away from us painlessly is a deliberate design choice.

items.q: How do we migrate from an old CRM or system without downtime?
items.a: Parallel run for 2–4 weeks: the new process runs alongside the old one until we're confident no data is lost and reporting isn't broken. Only then — the cutover.

items.q: How much does it cost and how do we calculate ROI before signing?
items.a: After the 30-minute audit — a range in EUR or AED for a defined scope, plus an ROI calculation (hours × loaded rate). Payment per stage. If the math doesn't add up, we don't sign.

items.q: Can we start with a single process without rebuilding the whole business?
items.a: That's the only way we work. The audit surfaces 5–7 spots — we start with one, either the most painful or the highest-ROI. The rest comes later, if the first one delivers. No "you have to automate everything at once or it won't work" — that's the pitch of people selling a box.

items.q: Does this fit our size or industry?
items.a: We work with SMB plus public sector. For micro (under 5 people) we often talk you out of it — the math doesn't work. If your case isn't ours — we'll say so and point you at someone who'll do it better.

items.q: Where are you based — and do you work on-site or remotely?
items.a: Home base is Dubai. We meet in person in Dubai Marina or at your office around the city; for remote clients — Zoom. Audit, prototype and support run from Dubai; larger rollouts with your team run hybrid.

items.q: What languages do you work in?
items.a: Russian and English are primary. The AI employees we build can reply to your customers in any language (including Arabic). Contracts and SLAs — bilingual on request.

items.q: Can we invoice through a UAE entity in dirhams?
items.a: Yes. Contract and invoice through our UAE entity, payment in AED, EUR or USD. For CIS clients we can arrange an alternative scheme if needed. Closing documents are VAT-compliant.

faq.contact: Question not on the list — message us on Telegram @Magicworkpro, no form needed.

contact.title: Leave a contact — we'll get back within 24 hours
contact.subtitle: Just your name and Telegram/phone is enough — the rest we'll cover on a call. Or message us directly on Telegram — that's the fastest path.
contact.success: Got it. We'll get back to you shortly.
contact.error: Something went wrong. Message us on Telegram instead.
